content:
- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and read the choose-task rules plus the list-active-tasks skill.
- Confirmed .ralph/current_tasks.md contains exactly one active candidate: story-29 task 06 with <passes>false</passes>.
- Inspected the task file header/tags and found no <blocked_by> or <priority> tag on the active task, and no active failing bug tasks remain; next step is to write this task path into .ralph/current_task.txt and quit.

- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with the selected active task path.
- Verified the only active unblocked failing task is .ralph/tasks/story-29-migrate-to-nix-crane/06-task-fix-nix-ci-cd-artifact-reuse-and-cache-speed.md.
- Next step after this chooser exits is to execute that story-29 CI/Nix task and validate make check, make test, and make lint after implementation.

- Loaded prior progress, current task path, and the required tdd / improve-code-boundaries skill docs.
- Confirmed the active task is story-29 task 06 about fixing Nix CI/CD artifact reuse and cache speed.
- Next step is to read the task body and adjacent story files, determine whether it is in planning or execution mode, and then either create/update a plan or execute it.

- Read the task body and the existing task-06 plan, which is already marked NOW EXECUTE.
- Confirmed this turn should implement the plan, using real Nix/workflow verification instead of fake YAML tests.
- Next step is to inspect the current publish/promote workflows and flake surfaces to map the planned build/test/image/publish boundaries onto the existing code.

- Inspected the current partial implementation in flake.nix, scripts/nix_ci_artifacts.py, and publish/promote workflows.
- Found likely immediate issues before any hosted verification: the cache action is pinned to main instead of v13, and the workflow uses suspicious artifact action versions that need validation.
- Next step is to verify with local Nix eval/check surfaces and then patch the workflow/helper/flake code to the minimal correct shape before doing hosted evidence collection.

- Confirmed the flake-owned CI catalog evaluates locally, so the next truth source is hosted GitHub Actions behavior rather than missing Nix attrs.
- Began hosted baseline verification via the authenticated GitHub API and am tracing the underlying flake outputs that the workflow consumes.
- Next step is to identify the latest hosted run outcome/timing plus any log-visible rebuild or syntax failures, then patch the workflow/helper with the smallest honest fix set.

- Used the latest hosted run as the baseline and found the real failing slice: the arm64 build-platform-bundle job dies after ~5m49s while amd64 is still building.
- Local Nix reproduction also shows a wrong-system boundary problem in the ci-build-bundle surface: x86-oriented derivations are being pulled into the wrong platform build graph.
- Next step is to inspect the runtime/image definitions feeding ci-build-bundle and patch the flake/workflow boundary so each platform bundle only contains artifacts native to that platform.

- Reproduced the hosted arm64 failure locally with a tiny nixpkgs#hello bundle: build-bundle crashed on KeyError(metadata).
- Patched scripts/nix_ci_artifacts.py to normalize optional metadata and updated publish-images to pin Magic Nix Cache to v13 with GitHub cache enabled and FlakeHub disabled.
- Next step is to verify the helper tracer bullet now passes, then run the repo checks and inspect the remaining workflow surfaces for any additional hosted-only blockers.

- Fixed the immediate hosted crash in scripts/nix_ci_artifacts.py and widened publish-images so workflow_dispatch can exercise the full publish/audit path for branch verification.
- Started the required local gates: make check plus actionlint on the workflow files.
- Next step is to resolve any check/lint failures, then run make lint and make test before pushing and collecting fresh hosted evidence.

- make check and actionlint are green; make lint is also green.
- make test is still running, and once it passes I will push the branch and start a fresh hosted publish-images workflow_dispatch run that can exercise the full publish/audit path.
- Next step after the local gates is hosted verification via GitHub Actions API, including build/image/publish/audit timing and cache evidence.

- All required local gates are green: make check, make lint, make test, plus actionlint on both workflow files.
- Pushed commit a928c3f to origin/codex-task06-verify and started a fresh hosted publish-images workflow_dispatch run for branch verification.
- Next step is to watch that hosted run through build, validate, image, publish, audit, and manifest jobs, then record timing and artifact-reuse evidence in the task notes.

- Fresh hosted run 25040438562 for commit a928c3f started at 2026-04-28T07:43:56Z on codex-task06-verify.
- Initial polling shows publish-catalog is running cleanly; next meaningful checkpoint is whether both build-platform-bundle jobs start and avoid the prior metadata crash.
- Next step is continued hosted polling, then log extraction for any remaining failure or for successful artifact/timing evidence.

- Hosted run 25040438562 passed the first important checkpoint: both amd64 and arm64 build-platform-bundle jobs started under magic-nix-cache-action@v13, and arm64 did not fail immediately on KeyError(metadata).
- The workflow is now in the long bundle-build phase; the next meaningful checkpoint is whether both bundle jobs complete and fan out into validate/image/publish.
- Next step is continued hosted polling and log inspection for the first remaining failure or, if green, timing/artifact evidence collection.

- Hosted run 25040438562 is still in the long platform-bundle build phase on both amd64 and arm64.
- No new state transition has happened yet; the next decision point is whether those jobs complete or fail around the prior 5-6 minute mark.
- Next step is immediate job-log extraction on the first non-success transition, or continued evidence collection if the bundle stage finishes green.
